commit.message:
[MLAS] Enable BF16 KAI SBGemm on NCHWc ARM builds (#27703)

### Description
Allow using KAI SBGemm on any ARM64 build (previously it needed the
condition that NCHWc be not enabled as the logistics of using the KAI
SBGemm path for the SBGemm based Conv was not clear). This change
instead allows using the KAI path for other call sites (by enabling it
in the build) but explicitly disallows using the KAI path only for the
Conv and continue using the default MLAS path for the same (as before).



### Motivation and Context

https://github.com/microsoft/onnxruntime/pull/26773#discussion_r2931357688

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: onnxruntime
repository.description: ONNX Runtime: cross-platform, high performance ML inferencing and training accelerator

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Improve: increase type provider cache timeout from 30s to 5 minutes (#329)

* Improve: increase type provider cache timeout from 30s to 5 minutes

Large OpenAPI schemas (e.g. Stripe's ~3MB spec) can take 30-50 seconds
to generate types. With a 30-second expiration, the cache entry's
invalidation timer fires *before* the Lazy<T> is evaluated, causing
the cache to expire shortly after the entry is created. This means
the IDE must regenerate types on every refresh cycle for large schemas,
defeating the purpose of the cache.

Increasing the timeout to 5 minutes:
- Allows large-schema users to benefit from caching across IDE refreshes
- Still allows schema changes to take effect within 5 minutes
- Applies to both OpenApiClientProvider and SwaggerClientProvider

Relates to #185.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

---------

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add shared fixtures and improve test infrastructure (#105)

- Add project-wide tests/conftest.py with common path fixtures,
  seed reset, mocks, and skip marks (skip_if_missing_testdata,
  skip_if_no_gpu)
- Implement --use-gpu pytest option via pytest_addoption and
  use_gpu fixture so the gpu_compatible marker is functional
- Guard all CUDA calls in reset_seed behind torch.cuda.is_available()
  to prevent failures in CPU-only environments
- Add CLI-specific tests/mussel/cli/conftest.py with test_data_path_cli
  alias fixture
- Register gpu_compatible marker in pyproject.toml

Addresses review comments:
- No unused heavy imports at collection time
- --use-gpu flag properly registered via pytest_addoption
- CUDA API calls guarded for CPU-only torch installs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Updated DB schema to include PaytmTxnToken on Orders table

- Updated PaymentService project structure comments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: initial implementation of KG testing harness

- 4 graph types: Simple, Hierarchical, Multi, WeightedGraph
- 5 multi-turn test scenarios (54 total turns)
- Ollama client with qwen3.5:4b + thinking token stripping
- Context manager with sliding window (forces KG use)
- KG-augmented agent with label/tag search and neighbor expansion
- Test runner with keyword accuracy evaluation
- Markdown reporter for per-run and summary reports
- Research matrix builder for full parameter sweep
- Initial quick test: 98.3% accuracy across 54 turns

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: create Cancelled notification when payment link expires on visit

When a user visits an expired payment link, ExpireOrderAndRestoreCartAsync
cancels the order but previously did not create an admin notification.
The background cleanup service would then skip the order since it was
already cancelled, resulting in no Cancelled notification at all.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

PCS bicep cleanup & generalization of PCS CLI (#6112)

commit.message:
fix: cache Paytm txnToken to prevent 'Repeat Request Inconsistent' on retry

When a user visits the payment page, presses back on Paytm, then
re-clicks the payment link, Paytm rejected the 2nd initiation with
error 2023. Now the txnToken from the first initiation is cached on
the Order and reused for subsequent visits within the same payment
window.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: refresh notifications on bell click + hide skip-to-content link

- Bell click now re-fetches unread from API (catches notifications
  that arrived between login and bell click)
- Skip-to-content link properly hidden off-screen (was visible on
  login page due to top:-100% not working reliably)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: add diagnostic logging for notification fetch/create

Helps debug missing notifications after login — logs both
backend (creation + fetch count) and frontend (success/error).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: skip chatbot for unsupported WhatsApp message types

Unknown message types (reaction, sticker, image, system, etc.) were
falling through to the default case which set textBody='menu', causing
the welcome message + main menu to appear unexpectedly when clicking
interactive buttons like 'Add to Cart'.

Now unknown types are ignored instead of being treated as menu commands.

commit.message:
fix: WebAuthn registration for iOS passkeys (Face ID)

- Set ResidentKey to 'preferred' for discoverable credential support
  (required for iOS Passwords/Keychain passkey storage)
- Auto-detect request origin and add to RPOrigins dynamically so
  the origin validation passes when accessing from mobile devices
- Support comma-separated WEBAUTHN_ORIGIN for multiple origins
- Pre-read request body to prevent double-consumption issue
- Include transports and authenticatorAttachment in attestation response
- Add structured error logging for WebAuthn ceremonies

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden phpunit setup and activate CI test job

- Activate test-application CI job (was commented out)
- Remove deprecated phpunit.xml.dist attributes
- Add tests/php/ExampleTest.php as working smoke test
- Use container phpunit directly in test:php script (no Composer wrapper)
- Restore yoast/phpunit-polyfills as explicit dep (WordPress test bootstrap requirement)
- Remove phpunit/phpunit and wp-phpunit/wp-phpunit (provided by container and polyfills)
- Simplify bootstrap.php: drop non-wp-env fallback, fail fast with clear message
- Update testing docs: wp-env-exclusive constraint, tests-cli rationale, configuration notes

* ci: run application tests after static analysis passes

Exit early benefit: avoid spinning up Docker/wp-env when PHPStan
or lint already failed. Cache keys still resolve via needs: setup.

* docs: update github-actions doc with test-application job and stage structure

* chore: fix gitignore and distignore for phpunit artifacts

- Add phpunit.xml and .phpunit.result.cache to .gitignore
- Add phpunit.xml to .distignore
- Remove stale Cypress test entries from .gitignore

* docs: add plugin dependencies section to testing guide

* feat: add application-testing skill for writing PHPUnit tests

* chore: remove redundant namespace lookup guidance from testing skill

* docs: add application-testing skill to work-with-ai, add factory rollback note to testing

---------

commit.message:
feat: extract HR zones, TRIMP and strain from Garmin data

The sync script now extracts hrTimeInZone_1-5, activityTrainingLoad,
and computes TRIMP scores from each activity. Also adds a backfill
function that re-processes stored raw_garmin_data JSON for activities
that were synced before this change.

This fixes empty Zone Analysis, Trends strain chart, and Training
Load analytics — all of which require hr_zone_minutes, strain_score,
and trimp_score columns that were previously never populated.

Signed-off-by: Anil Belur <askb23@gmail.com>
Change-Id: I62e29686f4524e49a9778a0135fef81c

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Change-Id: I0fe4a0d5498229eda629379034a43fc83299b684

commit.message:
Address Pad Reflect vulnerability (#27652)

This pull request addresses a critical validation gap in the "reflect"
mode of the `Pad` operator for both CPU and CUDA backends, ensuring
compliance with the ONNX specification and preventing out-of-bounds
memory access. The main change is the addition of checks that prevent
the pad size from exceeding the maximum allowed value (`extent - 1`) for
each axis, and the introduction of comprehensive regression tests to
verify the new behavior.

Validation fixes for reflect-mode padding:

* Added explicit checks in
`onnxruntime/core/providers/cpu/tensor/pad.cc` and
`onnxruntime/core/providers/cuda/tensor/pad.cc` to ensure that, for
reflect mode, both pre-pad and post-pad values do not exceed `extent -
1` for each axis, as required by the ONNX spec. This prevents heap
out-of-bounds errors and aligns with numpy behavior.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b84d79dea8e316c5a5fec57854f215d103666c4f4754268c4eadbd814329f0d6L498-R499)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b84d79dea8e316c5a5fec57854f215d103666c4f4754268c4eadbd814329f0d6R510-R522)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-a6cea0925b036808544af30a643d0dddf27446dd4c1a68d67aba2d20b056bf70R198-R200)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-a6cea0925b036808544af30a643d0dddf27446dd4c1a68d67aba2d20b056bf70R215-R227)

Testing and regression coverage:

* Added a suite of regression tests in
`onnxruntime/test/providers/cpu/tensor/pad_test.cc` to verify that
invalid pad sizes in reflect mode are correctly rejected, including edge
cases for 1D and 2D inputs, boundary conditions, and scenarios with
slicing. These tests ensure that the operator fails gracefully when pad
sizes exceed the allowed limit and succeeds when within bounds.

Other changes:

* Minor file encoding update in
`onnxruntime/test/providers/cpu/tensor/pad_test.cc`.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use garth native OAuth + MFA flow for Garmin auth

Previous auth code used garminconnect in a way that produced
'OAuth1 token is required for OAuth2 refresh' errors. Now:

- Uses return_on_mfa=True for proper MFA handling
- Saves tokens via garth.dump() (directory format) instead of

commit.message:
fix: rewrite /_next/ in ALL text responses, not just HTML

Previous approach only rewrote HTML and turbopack JS, leaving RSC
flight responses, regular JS chunks, and CSS with raw /_next/ paths.
When Next.js client-side navigation fetched RSC data, module refs
like /_next/static/chunks/xxx.js bypassed ingress -> 404 -> React
hydration failed with cascading errors.

Now rewrites /_next/ in all text-based responses (text/*, javascript,
json, x-component). Binary responses (fonts, images) pass through.

Also adds 20 automated E2E tests covering HTML, JS, CSS, RSC flight,
binary passthrough, API JSON, prefix stripping, and no-header cases.

Signed-off-by: Anil Belur <askb23@gmail.com>
Change-Id: I00f087bc1e884a0ab6c879a392938a8b

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Change-Id: Ia6e272db70dd9404380bb35966a9b709ccc2dda3

repository.name: ha-garmin-fitness-coach-addon
repository.description: Home Assistant addon — Garmin Connect sync + AI coaching dashboard

commit.message:
fix: rewrite /_next/ in turbopack runtime and CSS for ingress

Root cause: turbopack runtime JS has hardcoded `let t="/_next/"`
as the base path for ALL dynamic chunk loading. When the browser
loads chunks via this path, it requests `/_next/static/chunks/..."
directly from the HA host, bypassing ingress → 404 → React hydration
silently fails (useEffect never fires, no client interactivity).

Similarly, CSS files contain `url(/_next/static/media/...)` for fonts
which also bypass ingress.

Fix: extend the ingress proxy to also rewrite turbopack runtime JS
files and CSS files, replacing `/_next/` with the ingress prefix.

Signed-off-by: Anil Belur <askb23@gmail.com>
Change-Id: I3b47a848811f420c9072d225224c09e4

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Change-Id: I57c46c16264898b96cbd5d9a727755d25006249c

commit.message:
fix: use platform authenticator for WebAuthn to enable Face ID/Touch ID

Changed AuthenticatorAttachment from CrossPlatform (security keys only)
to Platform (biometrics built into the device). This ensures iOS Face ID,
Touch ID, and Android fingerprint are offered instead of prompting for an
external security key.

Note: existing credentials registered as cross-platform must be deleted
and re-registered after this change.

repository.name: hhh-frontend
repository.description: H³ - Main frontend (React) for route and contract management

commit.message:
feat: add refresh token auth and WebAuthn biometric login

- Replace 72-hour access tokens with 15-minute access + 30-day rolling
  refresh tokens for seamless session persistence
- Add POST /auth/refresh endpoint that issues new token pairs and
  revokes the old refresh token
- Add frontend axios interceptor that silently refreshes expired access
  tokens, queuing concurrent requests during refresh
- Implement WebAuthn/FIDO2 backend with go-webauthn library for
  Face ID, Touch ID, and fingerprint authentication
- Add registration and login ceremony endpoints under /auth/webauthn
- Add biometric login button to LoginPage with automatic detection
  of registered credentials and last-used username memory
- Add biometric credential management to SettingsPage (register,
  list, remove)
- Add RefreshToken and WebAuthnCredential database models
- Configure WEBAUTHN_RP_ID and WEBAUTHN_ORIGIN via environment variables
- Update Swagger types to include refreshToken in AuthResponse

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: coin-collection-app
repository.description: A simple application to track one's coin collection

commit.message:
Feature/add mvc commands (#187)

* Add MVC command structure and CLI tool for application scaffolding

- Introduced a new command interface and several command implementations, including `CreateAppCommand`, `InitializeBackgroundTasksCommand`, and `InitializeMigrationsCommand`, to facilitate application setup and management.
- Added a `ConsoleOutput` class for standardized console messaging, enhancing user feedback during command execution.
- Implemented a `StubGenerator` for generating application files from templates, improving the scaffolding process.
- Created a CLI entry point in `mvc` for executing commands, streamlining the command-line interface for users.
- Updated `composer.json` to include the new CLI binary.

These changes enhance the MVC framework's usability by providing a robust command-line interface for application development and management.

* Add migration commands and tests for MVC framework

- Introduced `MigrationsCreateCommand`, `MigrationsRunCommand`, and `MigrationsTestCommand` to facilitate migration management within the MVC framework.
- Implemented command functionalities for creating migrations, running pending migrations, and testing specific migrations with rollback capabilities.
- Added comprehensive unit tests for each command to ensure correct behavior and output, including validation for required parameters and error handling.
- Updated `MvcCli` to register the new migration commands, enhancing the command-line interface for users.

These additions significantly improve the migration management capabilities of the MVC framework, aligning with best practices for database operations and user experience.

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add SchemaReaderErrors property to OpenApiClientProvider (fixes #160) (#327)

* Add SchemaReaderErrors static property to OpenApiClientProvider (fixes #160)

Exposes parse errors collected during schema loading as a static
'SchemaReaderErrors : string list' property on the generated root type.

- Non-empty only when IgnoreParseErrors=true and the schema has issues
- Each entry formatted as '<message> @ <pointer>' (matches stderr output)
- Returns [] for valid schemas, safe to always inspect

Also adds Swagger.SchemaReaderErrors.Tests covering:
- Valid schema → empty list
- Schema with validation errors + IgnoreParseErrors=true → non-empty list
- Error entries contain both message and pointer parts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks
